name: THYROGLOSSAL DUCT CYST
definition: failure of complete involution of embryonic thyroglossal duct

demographics.Frequency: most common congenital neck mass (70% of all congenital neck anomalies); 2nd
demographics.Histo: cyst lined by stratified squamous epithelium / ciliated pseudostratified columnar
demographics.Age: < 10 years in 50%; 2nd peak at 20-30 years; M=F
demographics.Location: suprahyoid (15-25%), at level of hyoid (15-50%), infrahyoid (20-65%)

dominant_finding: homogeneous mucoid attenuation of 0-20 HU / occasionally higher ← increased protein
distribution: suprahyoid (15-25%), at level of hyoid (15-50%), infrahyoid (20-65%)
discriminating_features: cyst with occasional septation within 2 cm of midline
infrahyoid cyst embedded within / deep to strap muscle:
infrahyoid strap muscles beaks over edge of cyst
paramedian cyst with a tail that dives into hyoid bone

general: cyst with occasional septation within 2 cm of midline
infrahyoid cyst embedded within / deep to strap muscle:
infrahyoid strap muscles beaks over edge of cyst
paramedian cyst with a tail that dives into hyoid bone
US: hypoechoic mass with fine to coarse internal echoes (= proteinaceous material) + increased
SCINTIGRAPHY: uptake in functional thyroid tissue of thyroglossal duct cyst
CT: smooth well-circumscribed midline mass with thin wall
homogeneous mucoid attenuation of 0-20 HU / occasionally higher ← increased protein
tract of thyroid tissue between cystic mass + thyroid
MR: cyst of low to intermediate SI on T1WI + high SI on T2WI
nonenhancing / enhancing very thin rim (unless inflamed)
variable SI of fluid ← infection / hemorrhage

clinical.Cx: (1)  Infection → thick external wall ± internal septa
clinical.Rx: Sistrunk procedure (= resection of central portion of hyoid bone + core of tissue
